sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
ll operations (VFR, which is preferred, and IFR) are DAY ONLY.
IFR operation has specific mandatory RAIM requirements for non-SBAS aircraft; detailed in APPENDIX.
Netjets has authorisation to waive the CAT B/C 24-month LSZS landing recency requirement when simulator training
has been provided on a simulator approved by the National Aviation Authority; The LSZS PIC simulator training
qualification fulfils this.

PIC Qualification: PIC to be qualified by completing the LSZS online test and simulator training. LSZS PIC qualification
can be checked on ATMS and 'Waypoint' App - 'My Currency'.

SIC Qualification: SIC is qualified by having completed the LSZS online test. LSZS SIC qualification is NOT shown on
ATMS or 'Waypoint' App - 'My Currency'.

The validity of the online test and SIM training is 12months as per OM-A 5.4.1.2.10 Validity.

PF shall hold a current AHQ.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
10-10-2P2

-

(1) VFR in Class E requires 8KM visibility, 1000ft vertical, 1500m horizontal clearance from cloud.

(2) PDCS is used to check both the Landing and Balked Landing performance. The required PDCS settings are
described fully in the arrival procedure section.

(3) RNP RWY21 has a 15-degree offset with a very high ceiling requirement which permits a stable approach. CAT-
C aircraft are authorised to perform this approach using CDFA technique to the IFR charted minima on Jeppesen
chart 12-1. This procedure remains compliant with the intent of OM-A 8.3.28.18.3-part B.

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
amedan airport is situated in a mountainous area in eastern Switzerland. The Regional Airport, operated by Engadin
Airport, lies in the heart of one of the Alpine tourist regions. Located 5 km from St. Moritz at a height of 5600 ft above
sea level, it is the highest airport in Europe.

Overview

lines: amedan airport is situated in a mountainous area in eastern Switzerland. The Regional Airport, operated by Engadin
Airport, lies in the heart of one of the Alpine tourist regions. Located 5 km from St. Moritz at a height of 5600 ft above
sea level, it is the highest airport in Europe.

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
SZS is aligned with the valley and the Samedan FIZ has Class G airspace from ground to 2000ft AGL and Class E from
2000ft AGL to 10,000ft AMSL.

Samedan is open to VFR and IFR traffic, the latter is likely to have significant delays and comes with a performance
penalty. Due to this, VFR is preferred when weather permits. However, when operationally quiet and in good weather,
consider IFR for upkeep of procedural proficiency.

Plan and brief early the use of VFR vs IFR by checking the following: Confirm the suitability of weather and the flight
plan issued, then coordinate VFR vs IFR with Dispatch and or Samedan Info as required. Note that IFR / VFR require
reference to specific performance information and careful performance assessment and planning is required. The
relevant PDCS chart required is detailed in section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS Arrival and Departure tables.

The ATS flight plan code at the end of the OFP (reference OM-A 8.1.10.4) will show how you have been filed: For
example (FPL-NJE123A-IN) shows that NJE123A has been filed ‘I’ – intended entire flight opeated IFR. The other
options are ‘V’ – intended entire flight opeated VFR, ‘Y’ – intended initially IFR followed by change to VFR and ‘Z’ –
intended initially VFR followed by change to IFR.

Note the location of Shooting Range, Restricted area LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A on Jeppesen 19-11, and check if active by
NOTAM, Samedan Info or by calling +41 (0) 44 813 31 10.
All arrivals to Rwy 21 & 03 and departure from Rwy 03 require the range to be cold.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
10-10-2P5

-

WARNING:
Cold Temperature Correction; must be applied as per OM-A 8.3.3.6 whenever the airport is 0°C or colder. Due to this
being critical for IFR ops at LSZS, the appendix to this brief contains the calculated corrections.

VFR Waypoints:
Check the following waypoints are correctly inserted into the FMS, these are required for VFR and will help SA when
IFR:
ZERNEZ
ZERNZ
N46 42.0 E010 05.6
MALOJA
MALOJ
N46 24.0 E009 41.8

VFR transponder code; When VFR in Switzerland, squawk 7000

VFR TRAFFIC:
CAUTION:
Extensive VFR traffic – maintain constant sharp lookout and fly on RHS of valley whilst VFR.

Note the location of the VFR valley passes, reporting points and circuits. Expect VFR traffic to be concentrated in these
areas.
Note the location of St. Moritz – Expect possible heli traffic especially in winter.

CAUTION:
Glider / Hang/Para glider activity: The mountains and valleys surrounding the airport provide ideal gliding
conditions during the months of May to October. Expect high glider activity during these months. Gliders may not
be transponder equipped.

DAVOS World Economic Forum
All flights into LSZS during DAVOS WEF must use the procedures as described in the specific WEF pilot brief (activated
by NOTAM). They will be different to the procedures described in this OM-C brief which is to be used at all other
times.

WEATHER:
Expect possibility of quickly deteriorating weather during unstable conditions. Valley conditions can be confirmed
on Samedan website webcams. Average weather shown here and Cold weather correction tables are detailed
APPENDIX.

AIRFIELD WEBSITE: www.engadin-airport.ch

NOISE ABATEMENT:
Refer to Jeppesen 10-1P2 for noise abatement procedures on ground.
Refer to VFR Jeppesen chart 19-1A and note the noise sensitive areas. Prioritise Safety, however, try to avoid
overflying these areas when able.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
10-10-2P6

-
THREATS:

CFIT
-
Cold temperature correction is critical, especially during IFR procedures.
-
Increased probability of loss of RNP, especially critical during IFR approaches.
-
Precise visual manoeuvring within a valley is required. EGPWS hotspots detailed in APPENDIX.
-
High density altitude handling; increased turn radius, decreased aircraft performance.
-
Winter conditions – snow obscured terrain.
-
High deck angle on climb-out; reduced forward visibility.
-
Non-Standard PAPIs; For guidance only – does not give obstacle clearance.

RWY EXCURSION
-
High density altitude handling; destabilised approach and longer landing rollout.
-
Narrow RWY at 40m.
-
Landing Rwy 03 requires late manoeuvring that can be destabilising.
-
Non-Standard PAPI; 5° Left of Centreline, can be destabilising.
-
No Approach lights or RWY lights.

MID AIR COLLISION
-
Class E and G airspace; no separation between IFR/VFR or VFR/VFR.
-
Extensive GA VFR traffic year-round, hotspots at VFR mountain passes.
-
AFIS only; AFISO has no traffic display and is prohibited from sequencing airborne traffic.
-
Heliport nearby; hospital just west of Rwy, rotary traffic can trigger TCAS.
-
High intensity rotary activity around St. Moritz, especially in Winter.
-
Gliders active in FIZ during the summer; they may not be transponder equipped.
-
Glider area East of Zernez village, near ZS710.

LOSS OF CONTROL
-
Proximity to terrain can cause turbulence and wind-shear in light to moderate wind.
-
High density altitude handling; Increased turn radius can lead to overbanking and decreased stall margin.

RWY INCURSION
-
Short taxi out on narrow taxiway to active RWY.
-
No taxi lighting.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
10-10-2P7

-

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
When weather permits, the VFR arrival is preferred.
This section contains 9.2. VFR Arrivals and 9.3. IFR Arrivals.

9.2. VFR Arrivals
Reference the following:
A. Arrivals table under section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS for a list of approaches with associated
limitations and information. Only these approaches are authorised.
B. Section 8. OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES – GENERAL prior to all operations at Samedan.
C. Finally, see below operating instructions for VFR arrivals.

9.2.1. VFR APPROACH RWY 03 VIA MALOJ
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility: 8km.
CEILING:
With BKN or OVC cloud - 10,400ft AAL; FEW or SCT cloud - 2200ft AAL.
Pilots’ assessment required to ensure approach, RWY and missed approach are clear of cloud

Jeppesen Charts:
19-1, 19-1A, 19-11

Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 03. And,
‘Departure’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 03DP4.

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03. Input actual landing
weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’.
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03DP4.
Input actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-around configuration. ‘Options’ to
DRY RWY regardless of actual RWY state.

PROCEDURE:
A. Arrival:
-
Expect IFR routing with Zurich ATC to LSZS overhead descending to FL160. Note that there are three published
IFR holds in the vicinity of LSZS, these being at PELAD, GUGSA and RONAG.
-
Confirm the status of LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A (with Samedan Info secondary comm) whilst still IFR prior to arrival. If
either of these is active, then Samedan info will need to coordinate a crossing clearance with Swiss MIL.
-
When LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A is active and if no coordinated crossing clearance is available then this will prevent ALL
arrivals (due to approach RWY 21 and Missed approach RWY 03) - Remain IFR and either hold until able to enter
LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A or divert as necessary.

B. Overhead:
-
In the overhead at FL160 identify Samedan valley and airport. Confirm that you can proceed visually. VMC needs
to be maintained throughout the approach and possible missed approach. Check the whole length of the valley
between MALOJ and ZERNZ.
-
If unable to proceed visually then remain IFR and hold or divert as necessary.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
10-10-2P8

-
-
If conditions permit the VFR approach and possible missed approach, then cancel IFR.
-
Start descent to 12000ft AMSL and position for a straight in VFR approach via MALOJ for RWY 03.

C. Downwind Leg:
-
Be prepared to lose visual contact with the airport. Extend downwind leg until abeam MALOJ for RWY 03.

Overhead. North up.

VFR Chart -Downwind for RWY 03

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
10-10-2P9

-
D. Turning Final Rwy 03:
Start a left descending turn to final (min 1000 fpm is recommended).

E. Final Rwy 03:
Point ‘W’ is shown on the VFR Jeppesen charts, is 2nm final and the edge of Samedan FIZ if asked to report it.
There is a hill on final Rwy 03 centreline. Pass to the left of the hill for an offset final to prevent undue steep approach
or terrain proximity. Ensure Stable approach criteria by 300ft AAL.

F. Missed Approach / Balked Landing:
Visual manoeuvring will be required. Initially route as per PDCS OEI procedure then when performance allows proceed
visually to ZERNZ and out-climb the valley to 12000ft AMSL. Return to the LSZS overhead for a repeat VFR approach
or coordinate IFR diversion as required.

Final RWY 03.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P10

-
9.2.2. VFR APPROACH RWY 21 VIA ZERNZ
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility: 8km.
CEILING:
With BKN or OVC cloud - 10,400ft AAL; FEW or SCT cloud - 2200ft AAL.
Pilots’ assessment required to ensure approach, RWY and missed approach are clear of cloud.

Jeppesen Charts:
19-1, 19-1A, 19-11

Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 21. And,
‘Departure’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 21DP2.

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21. Input actual landing
weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’.
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21DP2.
Input actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-around configuration. ‘Options’ to
DRY RWY regardless of actual RWY state.

PROCEDURE:
A. Arrival:
-
Expect IFR routing with Zurich ATC to LSZS overhead descending to FL160. Note that there are three published
IFR holds in the vicinity of LSZS, these being at PELAD, GUGSA and RONAG.
-
Confirm the status of LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A (with Samedan Info secondary comm) whilst still IFR prior to arrival. If
either of these is active, then Samedan info will need to coordinate a crossing clearance with Swiss MIL.
-
When LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A is active and if no coordinated crossing clearance is available then this will prevent ALL
arrivals (due to approach RWY 21 and Missed approach RWY 03) - Remain IFR and either hold until able to enter
LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A or divert as necessary.

B. Overhead:
-
In the overhead at FL160 identify Samedan valley and airport. Confirm that you can proceed visually. VMC needs
to be maintained throughout the approach and possible missed approach. Check the whole length of the valley
between MALOJ and ZERNZ.
-
If unable to proceed visually then remain IFR and hold or divert as necessary.
-
If conditions permit the VFR approach and possible missed approach, then cancel IFR.
-
Start descent to 12000ft AMSL and position for a straight in VFR approach via ZERNZ for RWY 21.

C. Downwind Leg:
-
Be prepared to lose visual contact with the airport. Extend downwind leg until abeam ZERNZ for RWY 21.

D. Turning Final Rwy 21:
-
Start a left descending turn to final. Recommended rate 1000-1500fpm.

E. Final Rwy 21:
-
Point ‘E’ is shown on the VFR Jeppesen charts, is 2nm final and the edge of Samedan FIZ if asked to report it.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P11

-
F. Missed Approach / Balked Landing:
-
Visual manoeuvring will be required. Initially route as per PDCS OEI procedure then when performance allows
proceed visually to MALOJ and out-climb the valley to 12000ft AMSL. Return to the LSZS overhead for a repeat
VFR approach or coordinate IFR diversion as required.

VFR Chart -Downwind for RWY 21

Final RWY 21

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P12

-
9.3. IFR Arrivals
Reference the following:
A. Arrivals table under section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS for a list of approaches with associated
limitations and information. Only these approaches are authorised.
B. Section 8. OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES – GENERAL prior to all operations at Samedan.
C. Finally, see below operating instructions for IFR arrivals.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P13

-
9.3.1. VISUAL APPROACH WITH RNP GUIDANCE RWY 03

MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility 5000m. DA As per Jeppesen + 50ft.
CEILING:
5800ft AAL.
Jeppesen Charts:
19-10

Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 03. And,
‘Departure’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 03DP5.

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03. Input actual landing
weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’.
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03DP5.
Input actual weather. Input ‘Flap’, and ‘Options’ as per table below.

RAIM Prediction Check for Non-SBAS Equipped Aircraft:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Note the FMS based RAIM check is not valid. IFR operations on non-SBAS equipped
aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay, divert or coordinate
refiling as VFR as required.

Altimeter Cold Temperature Corrections:
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to all published altitudes shown on approach and
missed approach procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.

PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

There are 2 decision points with specific required visual references as follows:
Point 1 – DA as per Jeppesen; Either the aerodrome shall be in sight or both crew members shall be able to
positively identify terrain features that will enable the visual portion to be safely carried out as per OM-A 8.3.28.11
Visual Approaches Under IFR.
Point 2 – 8820ft AMSL; The landing runway shall be in sight at this NetJets defined point.

Required PDCS Settings for checking Balked Landing Performance on IFR Approach
PDCS
‘Departure’ to
LSZS
SETTING
EMB-505
CE-560XLS
CE-680A
BD-100
DA-2EASy
BD-700
‘Runway’ to
03DP5
‘FLAP’
2
15
2
10
10
6
‘ OPTIONS’
ANTI-ICE
Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder
and reported weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection
during any of the published missed approach procedure. (Rwy 03 - 14,000ft
at RONAG).
DRY RWY
Always Set to DRY regardless of actual RWY State.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P14

-
If at either decision point the required visual references are not met, or subsequently lost, perform the missed
approach procedure.
Once visual with RWY and having passed Point 2, continue as per VFR Approach - Final RWY 03.

Missed Approach / Balked Landing Procedure:
Immediately initiate a maximum gradient climb and follow the published missed approach procedure.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P15

-
9.3.2. RNP RWY 21
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility 5000m. DA As per Jeppesen + 50ft
Jeppesen Charts:
12-1,12-1A

Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 21. And,
‘Departure’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 21DP3.

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
C. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21. Input actual landing
weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’.
D. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21DP3.
Input actual weather. Input ‘Flap’, and ‘Options’ as per table below.

RAIM Prediction Check for Non-SBAS Equipped Aircraft:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Note the FMS based RAIM check is not valid. IFR operations on non-SBAS equipped
aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay, divert or coordinate
refiling as VFR as required.

Altimeter Cold Temperature Corrections:
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to all published altitudes shown on approach and
missed approach procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.

PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.
Required Visual References to continue approach: If at the decision altitude the landing runway is not visible or visual
contact is subsequently lost, perform the missed approach procedure.
Once visual with the Rwy and having passed DA, continue as per VFR Approach - Final RWY 21
CAUTION:
The distance from the decision point to RWY is significantly further than the 5000 m minimum. Be Go-Around
minded in marginal conditions.

Missed Approach / Balked Landing Procedure: Immediately initiate a maximum gradient climb and follow the
published missed approach procedure.
Required PDCS Settings for checking Balked Landing Performance on IFR Approach

to LSZS

CE-680A
BD-100
DA-2EASy
BD-700

03DP5

ANTI-ICE
Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder
and reported weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection
during any of the published missed approach procedure. (Rwy 21 – 16,000ft
at PELAD)
DRY RWY
Always Set to DRY regardless of actual RWY State.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P16

lines: When weather permits, the VFR arrival is preferred.
This section contains 9.2. VFR Arrivals and 9.3. IFR Arrivals.
A. Arrivals table under section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS for a list of approaches with associated
limitations and information. Only these approaches are authorised.
B. Section 8. OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES – GENERAL prior to all operations at Samedan.
C. Finally, see below operating instructions for VFR arrivals.
With BKN or OVC cloud - 10,400ft AAL; FEW or SCT cloud - 2200ft AAL.
Pilots’ assessment required to ensure approach, RWY and missed approach are clear of cloud
Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 03. And,
Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03. Input actual landing
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03DP4.
Input actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-around configuration. ‘Options’ to
DRY RWY regardless of actual RWY state.
Expect IFR routing with Zurich ATC to LSZS overhead descending to FL160. Note that there are three published
IFR holds in the vicinity of LSZS, these being at PELAD, GUGSA and RONAG.
Confirm the status of LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A (with Samedan Info secondary comm) whilst still IFR prior to arrival. If
either of these is active, then Samedan info will need to coordinate a crossing clearance with Swiss MIL.
When LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A is active and if no coordinated crossing clearance is available then this will prevent ALL
arrivals (due to approach RWY 21 and Missed approach RWY 03) - Remain IFR and either hold until able to enter
LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A or divert as necessary.
In the overhead at FL160 identify Samedan valley and airport. Confirm that you can proceed visually. VMC needs
to be maintained throughout the approach and possible missed approach. Check the whole length of the valley
If unable to proceed visually then remain IFR and hold or divert as necessary.
If conditions permit the VFR approach and possible missed approach, then cancel IFR.
Start descent to 12000ft AMSL and position for a straight in VFR approach via MALOJ for RWY 03.
Be prepared to lose visual contact with the airport. Extend downwind leg until abeam MALOJ for RWY 03.
Start a left descending turn to final (min 1000 fpm is recommended).
Point ‘W’ is shown on the VFR Jeppesen charts, is 2nm final and the edge of Samedan FIZ if asked to report it.
There is a hill on final Rwy 03 centreline. Pass to the left of the hill for an offset final to prevent undue steep approach
or terrain proximity. Ensure Stable approach criteria by 300ft AAL.
Visual manoeuvring will be required. Initially route as per PDCS OEI procedure then when performance allows proceed
visually to ZERNZ and out-climb the valley to 12000ft AMSL. Return to the LSZS overhead for a repeat VFR approach
or coordinate IFR diversion as required.
With BKN or OVC cloud - 10,400ft AAL; FEW or SCT cloud - 2200ft AAL.
Pilots’ assessment required to ensure approach, RWY and missed approach are clear of cloud.
Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 21. And,
Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21. Input actual landing
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21DP2.
Input actual weather, ‘Flap’ to most optimal go-around configuration. ‘Options’ to
DRY RWY regardless of actual RWY state.
Expect IFR routing with Zurich ATC to LSZS overhead descending to FL160. Note that there are three published
IFR holds in the vicinity of LSZS, these being at PELAD, GUGSA and RONAG.
Confirm the status of LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A (with Samedan Info secondary comm) whilst still IFR prior to arrival. If
either of these is active, then Samedan info will need to coordinate a crossing clearance with Swiss MIL.
When LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A is active and if no coordinated crossing clearance is available then this will prevent ALL
arrivals (due to approach RWY 21 and Missed approach RWY 03) - Remain IFR and either hold until able to enter
LS(R)-11 / LS(R)-11A or divert as necessary.
In the overhead at FL160 identify Samedan valley and airport. Confirm that you can proceed visually. VMC needs
to be maintained throughout the approach and possible missed approach. Check the whole length of the valley
If unable to proceed visually then remain IFR and hold or divert as necessary.
If conditions permit the VFR approach and possible missed approach, then cancel IFR.
Start descent to 12000ft AMSL and position for a straight in VFR approach via ZERNZ for RWY 21.
Be prepared to lose visual contact with the airport. Extend downwind leg until abeam ZERNZ for RWY 21.
Start a left descending turn to final. Recommended rate 1000-1500fpm.
Point ‘E’ is shown on the VFR Jeppesen charts, is 2nm final and the edge of Samedan FIZ if asked to report it.
Visual manoeuvring will be required. Initially route as per PDCS OEI procedure then when performance allows
proceed visually to MALOJ and out-climb the valley to 12000ft AMSL. Return to the LSZS overhead for a repeat
VFR approach or coordinate IFR diversion as required.
A. Arrivals table under section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS for a list of approaches with associated
limitations and information. Only these approaches are authorised.
B. Section 8. OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES – GENERAL prior to all operations at Samedan.
C. Finally, see below operating instructions for IFR arrivals.
9.3.1. VISUAL APPROACH WITH RNP GUIDANCE RWY 03
DAY ONLY. Visibility 5000m. DA As per Jeppesen + 50ft.
Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 03. And,
Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03. Input actual landing
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 03DP5.
Input actual weather. Input ‘Flap’, and ‘Options’ as per table below.
RAIM Prediction Check for Non-SBAS Equipped Aircraft:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Note the FMS based RAIM check is not valid. IFR operations on non-SBAS equipped
aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay, divert or coordinate
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to all published altitudes shown on approach and
missed approach procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.
There are 2 decision points with specific required visual references as follows:
Point 1 – DA as per Jeppesen; Either the aerodrome shall be in sight or both crew members shall be able to
positively identify terrain features that will enable the visual portion to be safely carried out as per OM-A 8.3.28.11
Point 2 – 8820ft AMSL; The landing runway shall be in sight at this NetJets defined point.
Required PDCS Settings for checking Balked Landing Performance on IFR Approach
Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder
and reported weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection
during any of the published missed approach procedure. (Rwy 03 - 14,000ft
Always Set to DRY regardless of actual RWY State.
If at either decision point the required visual references are not met, or subsequently lost, perform the missed
Once visual with RWY and having passed Point 2, continue as per VFR Approach - Final RWY 03.
Immediately initiate a maximum gradient climb and follow the published missed approach procedure.
DAY ONLY. Visibility 5000m. DA As per Jeppesen + 50ft
Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LSZS, ‘Runway’ to 21. And,
Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
C. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21. Input actual landing
D. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LSZS and ‘Runway’ to 21DP3.
Input actual weather. Input ‘Flap’, and ‘Options’ as per table below.

Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Note the FMS based RAIM check is not valid. IFR operations on non-SBAS equipped
aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay, divert or coordinate
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to all published altitudes shown on approach and
missed approach procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.
Required Visual References to continue approach: If at the decision altitude the landing runway is not visible or visual
contact is subsequently lost, perform the missed approach procedure.
Once visual with the Rwy and having passed DA, continue as per VFR Approach - Final RWY 21
The distance from the decision point to RWY is significantly further than the 5000 m minimum. Be Go-Around
Missed Approach / Balked Landing Procedure: Immediately initiate a maximum gradient climb and follow the
Required PDCS Settings for checking Balked Landing Performance on IFR Approach
Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder
and reported weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection
during any of the published missed approach procedure. (Rwy 21 – 16,000ft

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1. GENERAL
When weather permits, the VFR departure is preferred.
This section contains 10.2 VFR Departures and 10.3 IFR Departures.

10.2. VFR Departures
Reference the following:
A. Departures table under section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS for a list of departures with
associated limitations and information. Only these departures are authorised.
B. Section 8. OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES – GENERAL prior to all operations at Samedan.
C. Finally, see below operating instructions for VFR departures.

10.2.1. VFR DEPARTURE RWY 03
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility: 8km.
CEILING:
With BKN or OVC cloud - 10,400ft AAL; FEW or SCT cloud - 2200ft AAL.
Pilots’ assessment required to ensure approach, RWY and missed approach are clear of cloud
Jeppesen Charts:
19-1, 19-1A, 19-11

Performance:
As per PDCS 03DP4.

Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.

Contingency:
As per PDCS 03DP4 ‘Engine Out Procedure’. Note the OEI valley course reversal.

PROCEDURE:

-
Expect VFR departure with coordinated IFR clearance with Swiss / Zurich ATC
-
Check the valley in the direction of departure to ensure VFR conditions can be maintained for the departure and
OEI procedure.
-
Climb visually with max gradient climb to clear the valley.
-
Maintain VMC until clear of terrain and IFR clearance is obtained. Expect Swiss radar to be available passing
12000ft AMSL.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P17

-
10.2.1. VFR DEPARTURE RWY 21
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility: 8km.
CEILING:
With BKN or OVC cloud - 10,400ft AAL; FEW or SCT cloud - 2200ft AAL.
Pilots’ assessment required to ensure approach, RWY and missed approach are clear of cloud
Jeppesen Charts:
19-1, 19-1A, 19-11

Performance:
As per PDCS 21DP2.

Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.

Contingency:
As per PDCS 21DP2 ‘Engine Out Procedure’.

PROCEDURE:

-
Expect VFR departure with coordinated IFR clearance with Swiss / Zurich ATC
-
Check the valley in the direction of departure to ensure VFR conditions can be maintained for the departure and
OEI procedure.
-
Climb visually with max gradient climb to clear the valley.
-
Maintain VMC until clear of terrain and IFR clearance is obtained. Expect Swiss radar to be available passing
12000ft AMSL.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P18

-
10.3. IFR Departures
All four IFR departures are high performance SIDs that mandate RNAV1 capability and require immediate IFR climb.
Carefully note requirements and weather minima as per Jeppesen prior to departure.

High Performance RNAV SIDs:
All SIDs require high performance with minimum climb gradient as follows:
RONAG 1E
16.6% to 10,200 ft AMSL
RONAG 1V
10.0% to 10,600 ft AMSL
PELAD 1V
10.0% to 11,500 ft AMSL
PELAD 1W
15.6% to 10,700 ft AMSL

Crew shall brief the climb profile they will use giving due consideration to the following:
o Minimum SID climb gradient and noting the OEI procedure laterally follows the SID.
o Best gradient climb speed (example V2+10)
o Departure altitude as per ATC
o Weather conditions with minimum icing speeds
o OM-B / AFM limitations
o Passenger comfort considerations due to deck angle.

The table below can be used to assist crews in meeting these requirements:

IFR Departure Instructions. Reference the following:
A. Departures table under section 6. WEATHER / AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS for a list of departures with
associated limitations and information. Only these departures are authorised.
B. Section 8. OPERATIONAL PROCEDURES – GENERAL prior to all operations at Samedan.
C. Finally, see below operating instructions for IFR departures.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P19

-
10.3.1. RWY 03 SID RONAG 1E
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility 2000m.
CEILING:
IRS Equipped A/C (BD-100, DA-2EASy and BD-700) – No Ceiling Required.

Non-IRS Equipped A/C (EMB-505, CE-560XLS and CE-680A) – Ceiling 2,000ft AAL.
Jeppesen Charts:
10-3A

Performance:
As per PDCS 03DP2.
Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.

Note: Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder and reported
weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection during the SID to 14,000ft AMSL.

Contingency:
With OEI; Laterally follow the SID, accelerate as per PDCS ‘LO MSL’ and climb to MSA.

Raim Prediction Check:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Operations on non-SBAS equipped aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as
unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.

Altimeter Cold Temperature Corrections:
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to the published altitudes shown on the SID and OEI
procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.

PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

-
Ensure weather is at or above minima prior to departure.
-
Initial climb shall be with maximum gradient; then, after climb profile is assured, continue the IMC climb as per
High Performance RNAV SID guidance to the departure altitude as per ATC.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P20

-
10.3.2. RWY 03 SID RONAG 1V
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility 5000m.
CEILING:
4400ft AAL.
Jeppesen Charts:
10-3C

Performance:
As per PDCS 03DP3.
Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.

Note: Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder and reported
weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection during the SID to 14,000ft AMSL.

Contingency:
With OEI; Laterally follow the SID, accelerate as per PDCS ‘LO MSL’ and climb to MSA.

Raim Prediction Check:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Operations on non-SBAS equipped aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as
unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.

Altimeter Cold Temperature Corrections:
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to the published altitudes shown on the SID and OEI
procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.

PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

-
Ensure weather is at or above minima prior to departure; ceiling minima requirement ensures ‘visual ground
contact’ until 10000ft AMSL.
-
Initial climb shall be with maximum gradient; then, after climb profile is assured, continue the IMC climb as per
High Performance RNAV SID guidance to the departure altitude as per ATC.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P21

-
10.3.3. RWY 21 SID PELAD 1W
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility 2000m.
CEILING:
IRS Equipped A/C (BD-100, DA-2EASy and BD-700) – No Ceiling Required.

Non-IRS Equipped A/C (EMB-505, CE-560XLS and CE-680A) – Ceiling 2,000ft AAL.
Jeppesen Charts:
10-3

Performance:
As per PDCS 21DP1.
Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.

Note: Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder and reported
weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection during the SID to 14,000ft AMSL.

Contingency:
With OEI; Laterally follow the SID, accelerate as per PDCS ‘LO MSL’ and climb to MSA.

Raim Prediction Check:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Operations on non-SBAS equipped aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as
unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.

Altimeter Cold Temperature Corrections:
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to the published altitudes shown on the SID and OEI
procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.

PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

-
Ensure weather is at or above minima prior to departure.
-
Initial climb shall be with maximum gradient; then, after climb profile is assured, continue the IMC climb as per
High Performance RNAV SID guidance to the departure altitude as per ATC.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P22

-
10.3.4. RWY 21 SID PELAD 1V
MINIMA:
DAY ONLY. Visibility 5000m.
CEILING:
5100ft AAL.
Jeppesen Charts:
10-3B

Performance:
As per PDCS 21DP1.
Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.

Note: Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder and reported
weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection during the SID to 14,000ft AMSL.

Contingency:
With OEI; Laterally follow the SID, accelerate as per PDCS ‘LO MSL’ and climb to MSA.

Raim Prediction Check:
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;

unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.

Altimeter Cold Temperature Corrections:
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to the published altitudes shown on the SID and OEI
procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.

PROCEDURE: As per Jeppesen.

-
Ensure weather is at or above minima prior to departure; ceiling minima requirement ensures ‘visual ground
contact’ until 10600ft AMSL.
-
Initial climb shall be with maximum gradient; then, after climb profile is assured, continue the IMC climb as per
High Performance RNAV SID guidance to the departure altitude as per ATC.

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P23

IRS Equipped A/C (BD-100, DA-2EASy and BD-700) – No Ceiling Required.
Non-IRS Equipped A/C (EMB-505, CE-560XLS and CE-680A) – Ceiling 2,000ft AAL.
Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.
Note: Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder and reported
weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection during the SID to 14,000ft AMSL.
With OEI; Laterally follow the SID, accelerate as per PDCS ‘LO MSL’ and climb to MSA.
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Operations on non-SBAS equipped aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as
unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to the published altitudes shown on the SID and OEI
procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.
Ensure weather is at or above minima prior to departure.
Initial climb shall be with maximum gradient; then, after climb profile is assured, continue the IMC climb as per
High Performance RNAV SID guidance to the departure altitude as per ATC.
Use actual weather and conditions in ‘Options’. Use most optimal ‘Flap’ setting for Take-off.
Note: Anti-Ice ON performance analysis must be used when LSZS is 10°C or colder and reported
weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection during the SID to 14,000ft AMSL.
With OEI; Laterally follow the SID, accelerate as per PDCS ‘LO MSL’ and climb to MSA.
Non-SBAS equipped aircraft will require a dispatch produced RAIM Prediction Check with 10-degree mask angle;
procedures detailed in APPENDIX. Operations on non-SBAS equipped aircraft during times when RAIM is predicted as
unavailable is prohibited. If necessary, delay or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.
Must be applied when LSZS surface temperature is 0°C or colder to the published altitudes shown on the SID and OEI
procedure. Use the correction chart in the APPENDIX.
Ensure weather is at or above minima prior to departure; ceiling minima requirement ensures ‘visual ground
Initial climb shall be with maximum gradient; then, after climb profile is assured, continue the IMC climb as per
High Performance RNAV SID guidance to the departure altitude as per ATC.

sections.title: Changes since the last revision
sections.body:
riefing has been updated to display the new template.
Added AHQ requirement.
Climb gradients adjusted on some IFR SIDs.
Provide feedback to Lis-OpsTechnical@netjets.com

SAMEDAN, SWITZERLAND
SAMEDAN
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P24

-

APPENDIX
This section contains the following supplemental information:

lines: riefing has been updated to display the new template.
Climb gradients adjusted on some IFR SIDs.
This section contains the following supplemental information:

sections.title: Revalidation requirements
sections.body:
. EGPWS ALERTS
The following EGPWS alerts are common due to the specific terrain and operating procedures at Samedan.
Crew response to ALL EGPWS alerts is to be in accordance with OMA 8.3.5.

lines: The following EGPWS alerts are common due to the specific terrain and operating procedures at Samedan.
Crew response to ALL EGPWS alerts is to be in accordance with OMA 8.3.5.

sections.title: Initial qualification requirements
sections.body:
. TERRAIN CAUTION and related PULL UP WARNING - Final RWY 03 associated with the hill on short
final.

lines: . TERRAIN CAUTION and related PULL UP WARNING - Final RWY 03 associated with the hill on short

sections.title: Initial qualification requirements
sections.body:
RAIM prediction check is mandatory for IFR operations at LSZS when SBAS is not installed or available. The
Citation XLS or any other aircraft type with SBAS INOP must perform a RAIM prediction check with 10-degree
mask angle for IFR operations at LSZS.

Dispatch Requirement: Produce a RAIM prediction check with 10-degree mask angle using the following website
and settings: https://augur.eurocontrol.int/tool/ Mask Angle: 10° / Algorithm: FD / Selective Availability: Unaware
(OFF) / Integrity Level: APPROACH (RNP 0.3) for Approaches or Terminal (RNP 1.0) for SIDs / Barometric Aiding:
Off

This produces a list of times when RAIM is predicted as being unavailable with 10-degree mask angle at Samedan.

Crew Requirement: For non-SBAS available aircraft, check the RAIM prediction times as provided by dispatch. The
FMS based RAIM check is not valid at LSZS as it does not limit 10-degree mask angle.
No IFR procedure at LSZS shall be commenced during periods when RAIM is predicted as being unavailable. If
necessary, delay, divert or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.

(OFF) / Integrity Level: APPROACH (RNP 0.3) for Approaches or Terminal (RNP 1.0) for SIDs / Barometric Aiding:
This produces a list of times when RAIM is predicted as being unavailable with 10-degree mask angle at Samedan.
Crew Requirement: For non-SBAS available aircraft, check the RAIM prediction times as provided by dispatch. The
FMS based RAIM check is not valid at LSZS as it does not limit 10-degree mask angle.
No IFR procedure at LSZS shall be commenced during periods when RAIM is predicted as being unavailable. If
necessary, delay, divert or coordinate refiling as VFR as required.
